title: Scrap Mechanic

notes:
Must use Wine D3d11 to get the game working (to do that, right click on the game in your library, click on the "set launch options" button and add "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" (without quotes) into the text field).

The game runs well but with minor issues like the lights being rendered weirdly).

The most annoying thing is that the game often crashes without any reason...
title: Scrap Mechanic

notes: To make game work at all you must add "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" (without quotes) into launch options. Game runs at around 25-35 fps regardless of graphics settings, while in Windows with same hardware game runs perfectly (above 60fps) at highest settings and 16x anisotropic filtering. I didn't notice any graphical errors in the game while trying to make it run at acceptable framerate. I didn't test challenge mode, multiplayer or Steam workshop.
title: Scrap Mechanic

notes: Requires PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command% (otherwise "unable to find suitable graphics adapter"). 0.3.5 freezes when resetting a challenge or deleting something from the lift. 0.3.2 usually works without crashes. Dynamic lights must be disabled to prevent huge cones that obscure the view. Glass is not transparent.
title: Scrap Mechanic

notes: Game didn't start unless Wine D3D11 was enabled, framerate was up to twice as good as Windows, but all transparent textures are now rendered opaque.

notes: EXPERIMENTAL: To fix the visual glitches & improve performance (a lot) you could try this recent DXVK build with a fix for Scrap Mechanic. (Just in case you need proof: https://github.com/doitsujin/dxvk/issues/1116)

notes: Doesn't need Wine D3D11 anymore after Proton 4.11. To fix "unable to find suitable graphics adapter" you need to install the 32-bit Vulkan driver. After this it will run but eat up lots of CPU on all threads.

notes: Stutters every time the game writes data to the disk. Resolved by writing a script that loads the worlds into a ramdisk when the game is started and back every 30 seconds while it's running.
